title: Life is Colorful
subtitle: Save your father. Commit light crimes. Try not to become a demon. You know, choices.
specs: be good, or survive — pick one

gallery.caption: Young Joseph, the amulet, and two versions of how this could go.

body: Life is Colorful follows young Joseph, who just wanted to save his sick father. He goes looking for the one potion that can do it, runs into a witch (they're always around when you least need them), and gets himself cursed. Now his amulet demands he commit bad deeds to keep going — but do too many, and the colour literally drains out of his life until he turns into a demon. No pressure.

So the whole game is a tug-of-war with your own conscience. Be good and struggle, or take the easy dark path and slowly stop being yourself. You only live once — choose your actions carefully. It's a moral-balance RPG dressed up as a cursed little fairytale.

The thing I'm proudest of on this one isn't even mine to claim solo: every single 2D asset in the game was drawn by our own artists. Nothing bought, nothing borrowed. In a world of asset-store shortcuts, four people sitting down and making an entire game's worth of original art still feels like the right kind of stubborn.

I was on development — wiring up the curse system, the choice tracking, and the slow colour-drain that's basically the game's whole soul. It's one of my earlier team projects, and you can feel us figuring out how to actually finish a game together. We did. It still holds up.
